# Jim Delaney (ChatGPT Essentials for Business Productivity)

<!-- vimeo: 1111454119 | track: English (auto-generated) -->

Hi everyone. My name is Jim Delaney. I'm an adjunct instructor and I'm currently teaching a course called Artificial Intelligence and Chat, GBT Essentials for Business Productivity. Now, what's interesting about this course is it's part of a workforce development continuing education program, and that means a lot of my students come from all different backgrounds. Some are early career, some are more seasoned or experienced professionals. Others may be in transition, as well as I may have some small business owners. So it's a really unique group, and I think that presents the challenge, right? How do I bring concepts of AI to this audience, um, in ways that's relevant and practical based on their backgrounds? I, so a little bit about my teaching philosophy. I really like to start with the why, like, or almost start with the end in mind. What are the problems, the goals, objectives we're trying to solve for? Then what I'll do is I'll take a methodical approach with more on the how, which is basically demonstrating simple but relevant examples based on, on, you know, things that, again, everyday life or, or their workplace. Next, I like to really work in what I like to call comprehension through collaboration, which is a team exercise right before they do it individually, we work as a group, and the real power in that is getting sort of that diversity of thought from different students where they can kind of learn from each other in terms of how they think about AI both creatively and logically. And then finally give them a chance to apply it themselves in terms of their own context and needs. So that's a pretty typical process I follow. At the end of the course, we get into this custom GPT sort of capstone project, right, where we bring together all the skills and knowledge they've learned and apply it in this concept. So the very first example I like to share with my students is one that's very simple, but fun and easy to um, relate to, and it's, uh, as I like to call her Isabella Portobella, which is a custom GBT that represents an expert in Italian cooking and wine pairing with, of course, a touch of humor and a short temper. But this is a very simple example that gives students a basic concept of how a custom GPT can work, how you can integrate your own internal knowledge and content with the knowledge of the large language model and work into a more targeted approach, repeatable approach to using A custom GBT concept after demonstrating the use or example that I just shared. I then do a team exercise that really allows the group of students to work together to build their own custom GBT. And in this example, um, we crowdsource the idea of a sort of virtual youth sports coach to help volunteer coaches, um, learn about different games and develop their own, uh, drills and lesson plans as part of their, their new coaching career, if you will. And what was fun about this is again, we had the students do everything from the start, literally the logo, the name, um, the description of the custom GBT, and of course the instructions, right? And that's where that mix of creativity and logic and rules come in where they really start to establish an understanding of where and how custom GBTs can work for them. So we do this together and then finally allow them as individual students to build their own concepts. And I've had some really interesting, um, examples coming from the student population. One example I love to share is there's a working mother who spends a lot of time, um, pulling coupons off of online sites, um, for a food shopping and meal planning. We are able to create a routine with a custom GBT that does that dynamically and saves her a tremendous amount of time and effort where it goes out and pulls coupons and generates that actual shopping list and dinner plan based on for sale items, right? So a big win there. Another example is, um, a startup business handyman business completed a, a full sort of go to market strategy, developed a custom GBT for developing service offerings and a pricing strategy to help them target and compete with local handy, uh, man sort of, um, businesses within the area, right? So again, very practical examples, one for personal productivity, another for business, but those are the kind of examples that really, um, shine in the class and give students an opportunity to really imagine the possibilities with chat EBT. So in terms of student outcomes, I'd say there's two big things. Number one, being able to speak confidently about AI and the use of chat EBT. There. A lot of my students start with uncertainty and almost fear of using this tool, but coming out of this class and learning things in an inner fashion allows them to really feel comfortable about not only using it, but but speaking to others about it. And I think the second thing is it, it just improves their quality of life or workplace. And in a lot of cases it may be about saving time, but in other cases, it's very much about improving the quality of the outcomes they're, they're trying to create or decisions they're trying to solve for problems they're trying to solve. So, um, I think twofold, that's really the combination. It's a confidence and b um, capability and it's, it's been a great experience.
